stories.title: OpenAI Ships GPT-5.6 to the Public with Three Tiers: Sol, Terra, and Luna
stories.summary: OpenAI moved GPT-5.6 from government-gated preview to broad public availability. Sol (flagship) at $5/$30 per M tokens, Terra at $2.50/$15, Luna at $1/$6. Sol runs on Cerebras at 750 tok/s. ChatGPT Work merges Codex + browser for multi-hour agent jobs with Google Drive/Slack/Salesforce integrations. GPT-Live full-duplex voice mode launched.

stories.title: xAI Launches Grok 4.5 — Opus-Class Model at One-Third the Price
stories.summary: xAI launched Grok 4.5, a 1.5T-parameter model at $2/$6 per M tokens vs GPT-5.6 Sol at $5/$30. Trained on Cursor data (SpaceX-acquired coding tool). Ships inside Cursor on every plan. Musk calls it 'Opus-class but faster, more token-efficient and lower cost.'

stories.title: Meta Charges for a Model for the First Time — Muse Spark 1.1
stories.summary: Meta launched Muse Spark 1.1 via the new Meta Model API — its first paid model and first public API. Multimodal, agentic, 1M-token context. Priced at $1.25/$4.25 per M tokens with $20 free credits. Strategic shift from free-to-paid adds credible third API option alongside OpenAI and Anthropic.

stories.title: Cognition Ships SWE-1.7 — Sub-$2 Coding Model Near Frontier Performance
stories.summary: Cognition released SWE-1.7, RL-trained on Kimi K2.7, served via Cerebras at ~1000 tok/s in Devin agent. 42.3% on FrontierCode 1.1, 81.5% on Terminal-Bench 2.1, at ~$1.97/task. Near-frontier coding at fraction of Opus 4.8 cost changes economics of automated review and bug fixing.

stories.title: UK AI Safety Institute Finds Universal Jailbreaks in GPT-5.6 Cyber Safeguards
stories.summary: UK AI Security Institute found universal jailbreaks in GPT-5.6 Sol within hours, unlocking long-form agentic work in vulnerability discovery and exploit development. Called 'among the highest-stakes safety issues of any model release so far.' Echoes cyber issue that triggered export controls on Anthropic's models weeks earlier.

stories.title: Anthropic Publishes J-Space Research — Inside a Model's Reasoning Workspace
stories.summary: Anthropic released interpretability research on 'J-space,' a small internal workspace under 10% of Claude's activations holding concepts during multi-step reasoning. J-lens technique allows inspection as token-like concepts. When suppressed, complex reasoning collapses while ordinary conversation continues. Pattern showed up across dozens of open models.

stories.title: China Labels Claude Code a Security Risk; Anthropic Confirms Hidden Tracking Code
stories.summary: China's MIIT warned Claude Code contained a 'backdoor' sending user location/identity to remote server. Anthropic confirmed embedded location-tracking to detect unauthorized model distillation. Alibaba ordered employees to stop using Anthropic tools from July 10. Raises data-governance concerns around anti-distillation measures.

stories.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secrets Allegations
stories.summary: Apple sued OpenAI in federal court in Northern California, alleging OpenAI recruited former Apple hardware staff and encouraged them to bring confidential designs, prototypes, and supplier data. Raises legal stakes around talent mobility between two of AI's most consequential companies.

stories.title: Anthropic's Secondary Market Valuation Passes OpenAI at $1.2 Trillion
stories.summary: Anthropic shares traded on secondary markets at implied $1.2T valuation in early July, above OpenAI's ~$908B. Company filed confidentially for IPO in June. Secondary-market mark reflects scarcity-driven demand pressure more than settled fundamentals.

stories.title: OpenAI Retracts SWE-Bench Pro — 30% of Tasks Found Broken
stories.summary: OpenAI audited SWE-Bench Pro, one of the most-cited coding benchmarks, and found ~30% of tasks broken. Automated pipeline flagged 27.4%, human reviewers 34.1%. Scores jumped from low 20s to ~80% in 8 months, suggesting benchmark gaming. Undermines main tool builders use to pick coding models.

stories.title: Tencent Releases Hy3 — 295B Open-Source MoE Model Under Apache 2.0
stories.summary: Tencent released Hy3, a 295B mixture-of-experts model (21B active, 256K context) under Apache 2.0 license. Free on OpenRouter through July 21. Major open-source release from Chinese lab adds to growing list of capable open models.

stories.title: Mistral Releases Robostral Navigate — Single-Camera Robot Navigation Model
stories.summary: Mistral released Robostral Navigate, an 8B robot-navigation model running on single RGB camera with no LiDAR or depth sensors. State-of-the-art seamless navigation from minimal onboard sensing. Represents significant advance in practical robotics AI.

stories.title: Nvidia Becomes Financier of AI Buildout with GPU Revenue Guarantees
stories.summary: Nvidia began backstopping GPU rental revenue for neocloud providers with six-year minimum-revenue guarantees. SemiAnalysis says structure could support AI-related debt market above $7T by 2029. Shifts bottleneck from raw compute to financing structure and credit risk.

stories.title: AI Adoption Linked to 10% Headcount Growth, 12% Entry-Level Hiring Increase
stories.summary: Ramp and Revelio Labs study across 21,559 US firms found heavy AI investors grew headcount ~10% in two years post-adoption, with entry-level hiring up ~12%. Gains concentrated in high-intensity adopters ($33/employee/month). Growth extended beyond engineering into sales, admin, CS, finance, marketing.

stories.title: Anthropic Documents Cost-Routing Pattern: 96% of Fable 5 Performance at 46% Cost
stories.summary: Anthropic published two Claude Managed Agents patterns ('Fable as Advisor' and 'Fable as Orchestrator') routing most steps on cheaper Sonnet 5, only high-uncertainty decisions to Fable 5. Achieves ~96% of Fable 5 performance at 46% cost on research tasks. Formalizes matching model tier to step complexity.
